items.rationale: Seen as crypto's clearest product-market fit: cheapest way to send a dollar, volumes rivaling Visa/ACH, organic use uncorrelated with trading, now with legal clarity (GENIUS Act).
items.age_note: Building since 2024-08 ('killer app'), intensifying through 2025; GENIUS Act 2025-07 cemented it

items.rationale: AI agents need crypto-native financial rails and identity; blockchains supply payments, provenance, proof-of-personhood, and trustworthy human feedback data. Repeatedly funded (Catena, Yupp, Poseidon, Ambient, EigenCloud).
items.age_note: Growing theme from 2024; accelerated sharply 2025 with x402 and agent-payment investments

items.rationale: Crypto networks as the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies; user-generated services for the physical world. Backed via Daylight (energy) and repeated framework-building.
Recently reframed as the single biggest friction/dealbreaker for institutional adoption, not just a moral good; many projects (Aztec, Aleo, Seismic, Miden, Railgun) cited as reaching maturity.
items.age_note: Privacy long valued; conviction escalated notably in 2025 (institutional framing 2025-09)

items.rationale: The house's preferred token model: value accrues to a decentralized network (not a company) via fees/burns; new regulatory clarity finally lets tokens 'complete the economic loop.'
items.age_note: Crystallized 2025 ('era of network tokens with decentralized onchain revenue is here', 2025-08)

items.rationale: Viewed as the endgame for scaling and a new developer primitive that democratizes SNARKs; falling prover overhead expands applicable use cases. Tempered by security/performance caveats (see concerns).
items.age_note: Strong since Jolt launch 2024-04; sustained through 2025 with realism added

items.rationale: DEX/CEX ratio rising, institutional rails arriving; with regulatory clarity DeFi can shed defensive 'lawyer-led product design.' Backed Legend, Universal, Halliday.
Named among categories 'obviously cooking'; expectation that traditional securities markets come onchain in 2025 (with securities laws applying).
Open protocols (Farcaster/Snapchain) enabling user-owned identity, data portability, and platform-competition; long-held part of the consumer-crypto thesis.
items.age_note: Recurring 2024-2025; less front-of-mind than payments/AI by late 2025

items.rationale: Held to extract value, deter serious builders, and arm hostile regulators — damaging crypto's long-term legitimacy. Acknowledged legal as non-securities but viewed as inherently valueless.
items.age_note: Strident in 2024; persists into 2025 ('gambling') though tone moderated as SEC clarified they aren't securities

items.rationale: Tokens whose value derives from a controlled company carry the same risks as securities; conflating them with network tokens would blow a hole in securities law and invite blowups.
items.age_note: Central thesis of 2025 token-taxonomy work (2025-03 onward)

items.rationale: Despite enthusiasm, zkVMs are riddled with bugs, lack formal verification, and remain >100,000x slower than native — dangerous to deploy in high-stakes systems prematurely.
items.age_note: Security concern voiced 2024-05; sharpened into explicit 'not ready for prime time' 2025-03

items.rationale: Unfettered access to secondary markets lets insiders hype-launch-dump on retail, breeding get-rich-quick schemes (SPAC analogy); disclosure alone won't fix it — insiders must be locked up until decentralization.
items.age_note: Repeated 2024-09 through 2025; embedded in CLARITY transfer-restriction advocacy

items.rationale: Used as decentralization theater and a shortcut; operationally inefficient, tax-risky, and controlled by foreign lawyers who don't understand the tech — their era should end with new policy/DUNA tooling.
items.age_note: Escalated through 2025 ('era of crypto offshoring is over', 2025-08)

items.rationale: PubCo wrappers holding less-mature, volatile tokens face unsolved disclosure, Gary-Plastic, and trade-below-NAV problems — manageable only for BTC/ETH/SOL, risky when teams spin up their own.

PBS decentralizes validation but centralizes block building ('proof of MEV'); exclusive order flow creates big-get-bigger dynamics, with multi-block/cross-chain MEV making it worse. Caution against rushing to regulate.

Years of SEC/IRS 'regulation by enforcement,' debanking, and expansive rules (broker, dealer) seen as anti-tech lawfare. Largely receding by late 2025, but post-Tornado-Cash ruling warns against handing regulators open-ended discretion.
items.age_note: Dominant fear 2024; substantially cooled in 2025 as clarity arrived, but not gone

items.rationale: Canonical 'network token': value accrues via EIP-1559 burn, expected long-run deflation, decentralized and not company-controlled — the model for what a token should be.
Praised for pragmatic, ship-focused engineering culture and growth into high-throughput financial-markets infrastructure; cited approvingly alongside ETH as a network token.
Core of the payments thesis; product-market fit, ATH volumes, now legally clear under GENIUS Act; Circle's IPO cited as maturity signal.
Direct conviction investment ($70M additional with lockup); EigenCloud seen as enabling verifiable, credibly-neutral apps and AI-agent infrastructure.
Conviction investment ($55M in ZRO with 3-year lockup); LayerZero seen as enabling new businesses and complex workflows to move onchain.
Repeatedly held up as a clean example of a network token that can capture protocol fees (via UniStaker) without being a security; not controlled by a company.
Respected as the most secure, decentralized network with genuine store-of-value characteristics distinct from memecoins; ETP flows cited as institutional-maturity signal.
items.age_note: Note ossification critique: upgrades frozen, but valued for security

items.rationale: The recurring cautionary archetype: economic reality was a synthetic profits-interest in FTX, so it should have been (and economically was) a security regardless of lacking formal legal rights.
items.age_note: Defunct; used illustratively throughout 2024-2025 as the anti-pattern

items.rationale: Pure speculation vehicles with no inherent value; ownership of nothing versus something — distinct from equity and from network tokens, and corrosive to the industry's credibility.

Tokens for blockchain systems that remain unilaterally controlled (e.g., team holding ~90% supply) carry securities-like trust dependencies and enable rug pulls; cited re a centralized token that 'did not' remove trust dependencies.

risk_regime.rationale: As of 2025-09-30 the house is the most optimistic it has ever been: regulatory clarity arriving (GENIUS Act signed, CLARITY/market-structure momentum, SEC no-action relief and constructive guidance), institutional adoption and ETP flows rising, stablecoin volumes at all-time highs, and infrastructure finally ready for consumer apps. The dominant 2024 fear — regulatory lawfare — has substantially receded. Posture is 'keep building / sales era,' tempered only by insistence on real value, decentralization, security, and not repeating speculative blowups.

notes: Recency weighting applied throughout. Major reversals/shifts recorded: (1) Overall regulatory posture flipped from defensive risk-off (2024: debanking, SEC enforcement, geofencing the US) to confident risk-on (2025: GENIUS Act, CLARITY) — the single biggest mood change in the corpus. (2) Offshore foundations went from accepted necessity to 'era is over' (2025). (3) zkVMs hold dual status: structurally exciting yet explicitly 'not ready for prime time' on security/performance — both views are current and held simultaneously. (4) Memecoin stance softened slightly in legal framing (SEC confirmed non-securities) but the disdain persists. The corpus reflects at least two distinct voices (a policy/legal voice heavily focused on decentralization-as-control and securities law; a research/technical voice on SNARKs, MEV, consensus; and a markets/infra voice on stablecoins, DePIN, AI) — treated here as one 'house consensus' but the token-taxonomy/decentralization thesis is the clearest unifying through-line. Conviction scores are intensity, not probability. Many sector entries marked 'grounded' rest on dated verbatim quotes; 'persisted' used for decentralized-social which is held but less recently emphasized than payments/AI.
